description:
## Component: hx-button-group (NEW)
**Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 4/6 libraries**

Groups related buttons with shared borders and spacing. Used for segmented controls, toolbar groups, and related action sets.

### ADR-001 Classification: Slot-Driven (Composite)
Container component. Drupal controls which buttons appear via slots. Component provides layout and visual grouping.

### API Design

**Properties:**
- `orientation`: horizontal | vertical
- `hx-size`: sm | md | lg (applies to all children)

**Slots:**
- Default slot: Accepts hx-button and hx-icon-button children

**CSS Parts:**
- `group`: Container element

### Drupal Integration
```twig
{# Toolbar actions in patient record #}
<hx-button-group>
  <hx-button variant="primary">Save</hx-button>
  <hx-button variant="secondary">Cancel</hx-button>
  <hx-button variant="ghost">Reset</hx-button>
</hx-button-group>
```
Content is fully slot-driven — Drupal TWIG templates control button order, count, and variants. Component provides visual cohesion (shared borders, border-radius on first/last child).

### Acceptance Criteria
1. Buttons share borders (no double borders between items)
2. First/last child get appropriate border-radius
3. Supports both horizontal and vertical orientation
4. `slotchange` listener updates layout when buttons added/removed dynamically
5. Size property cascades to child buttons via CSS custom properties
6. Works with mixed hx-button and hx-icon-button children
title: T1-03: hx-button-group — Grouped Action Buttons

Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In
`@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-button-group/hx-button-group.stories.ts`:
- Around line 72-75: The tests use non-null assertions (group!, shadowRoot!,
groupDiv!) which break strict-null checks; update the play tests in
hx-button-group.stories.ts (and the similar block at lines ~259-262) to avoid
"!" by first asserting existence and then safely accessing properties (e.g.,
await expect(group).toBeTruthy(); const groupDiv =
group?.shadowRoot?.querySelector('[part="group"]'); await
expect(groupDiv).toBeTruthy(); then assert role via
groupDiv!.getAttribute('role') or better access after the existence check) —
replace all direct non-null assertions on group, shadowRoot, and groupDiv with
sequential existence assertions or optional chaining followed by explicit
expects so the test fails visibly in CI if elements are missing.

In
`@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-button-group/hx-button-group.styles.ts`:
- Around line 25-27: The styles use hardcoded fallback lengths (e.g., "1px",
"0.375rem") in the CSS custom property fallbacks for selectors like
.group--horizontal ::slotted(*:not(:first-child)) and the other grouped rules
around lines noted; replace those raw literals with design-token variables (or
token-to-token fallbacks) only — for example swap occurrences of
var(--hx-border-width-thin, 1px) and similar fallbacks to a token-only
expression like var(--hx-border-width-thin, var(--hx-border-width-default)) or
another appropriate --hx-* token so no raw length literals remain in the
component CSS.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-button-group/hx-button-group.ts`:
- Around line 21-24: Add ElementInternals to the HelixButtonGroup component and
move the group semantics to the host: declare a private field (e.g., private

a pull request for branch "feature/t1-03-hx-button-group-grouped-action" into branch "dev" already exists:
https://github.com/bookedsolidtech/helix/pull/28

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: The play tests in stories.ts at lines 74 and 261 still assert `groupDiv?.getAttribute('role') === 'group'`. Since role was moved from the internal wrapper div to the host via ElementInternals, `[part="group"]` no longer carries `role="group"`. These assertions will fail or produce incorrect results. The reviewer's fix (check `groupDiv?.hasAttribute('role')` is false) correctly verifies the migration happened.

Looking at the current test.ts (lines 127-142), the requestUpdate spy is already in place: `vi.spyOn(el, 'requestUpdate')` and `expect(requestUpdateSpy).toHaveBeenCalled()` are both present. The feedback described what needed to be done, but it was already addressed. No action needed.

`(el as any).internals?.role` at line 168 of hx-button-group.test.ts violates the project's zero-any TypeScript rule. The reviewer correctly flags this. Since `internals` is private and `ElementInternals.role` does not reflect as an HTML attribute (confirmed by the CodeRabbit research), the test assertion should instead verify: (1) the host has no explicit `role` attribute (ElementInternals sets a default, not a DOM attribute), and (2) the internal wrapper div has no `role` attribute. This is the correct way to verify the ElementInternals pattern without using `any`.
